name: Larry The Lender
description_short: Family-owned hard money lender in Austin offering short-term real estate loans from $75K–$5M with in-house approval and 2-day closings.
description_long:
Larry The Lender is a family-owned hard money lending company founded and operated by cousins Larry and Mike, boasting over 50 years of combined lending experience in the Austin, Texas area. The company has expanded to serve multiple Texas locations including Dallas, Houston, San Antonio, and Spicewood, positioning itself as a direct lender specializing in asset-based real estate financing.

The company offers a range of short-term and bridge loan products primarily designed for real estate investors. Their services include hard money loans, asset-based lending, bridge loans, fix-and-flip financing, commercial hard money loans, and residential hard money loans. Loan amounts range from a $75,000 minimum to $5 million, with advertised rates starting at 12.99% APR. They market rapid closings—claiming 90% of loans fund within 2 days—and emphasize streamlined underwriting for time-sensitive real estate transactions.

Larry The Lender differentiates itself through several operational choices: all loan decisions are made in-house by the owners rather than external underwriters, they conduct their own property valuations using market comparisons instead of relying on traditional appraisals, they position themselves as direct lenders who do not use brokers, and they claim transparent, consistent terms with no hidden fees. The company emphasizes family legacy, swift processing, and direct owner involvement in all lending decisions.

However, potential borrowers should note that hard money loans carry significantly higher interest rates and fees than traditional mortgages, require substantial equity or collateral, involve shorter repayment terms (typically 6–24 months), and are designed for short-term real estate transactions rather than primary residence financing. These products are explicitly marketed to real estate investors, not homebuyers seeking traditional purchase or refinance mortgages.
pros: Fast closings: 90% of loans reportedly fund within 2 days with necessary title work
In-house underwriting and approval by company owners eliminates external delays
Direct lender model avoids broker markup and fees
Conducts own property valuations for faster approval vs. traditional appraisals
Serves multiple Texas markets (Austin, Dallas, Houston, San Antonio, Spicewood)
High loan amounts available up to $5 million for larger real estate projects
Specialized loan products for specific investor needs (fix-and-flip, bridge, commercial)
cons: Advertised rate of 12.99% is significantly higher than traditional mortgages (typically 6–8%)
Minimum loan amount of $75,000 excludes smaller borrowers
Hard money loans require short repayment terms (6–24 months), not suited for long-term financing
Designed for real estate investors and fix-and-flip projects, not primary residence buyers
Limited transparency on actual fees, closing costs, and loan terms beyond advertised rate
best_for: Real estate investors seeking short-term bridge or fix-and-flip financing
Commercial property developers needing quick capital for renovation or acquisition
Borrowers with substantial equity or collateral but limited traditional credit access
Time-sensitive real estate transactions requiring 2–7 day closings
diagnosis: Larry The Lender is best suited for experienced real estate investors and fix-and-flip entrepreneurs who need rapid capital for short-term projects and have substantial equity or collateral. The primary caveat is that hard money loans carry much higher costs than traditional mortgages and are not appropriate for primary residence buyers or long-term financing needs.
